~/day43$cat components.md
[ IN PROGRESS ] REACT BUILD

What I'm building.

// google keep — react edition · component architecture

NAVBAR
Navigation bar component logo · search · settings · profile icons — in its own Navbar/ folder
SIDEBAR
Collapsible sidebar component navigation links · icon + label layout — Sidebar/ folder
FORM
Note creation form title + text inputs · action icons — Form/ folder
NOTES
Notes grid + individual Note Notes.jsx renders a grid · Note.jsx is each card — data via props
[STATUS] in progress·5 components total
03/08
~/day43$cat learnings.log
[ DAY 43 ] REACT FUNDAMENTALS

What I learnt.

// 3 core concepts covered today

01
Components & exports each component lives in its own .jsx file · export default + named exports · import anywhere in the tree
02
Styling in React import .css directly into component file · use className instead of class · scoped per-component
03
Props & dynamic data pass data parent→child via props · access with props.value · display in JSX using { } curly braces
[PROGRESS] day 43 · zaio bootcamp | react
04/08
~/day43$cat App.jsx
[ CONCEPT ] COMPONENTS & IMPORTS

Splitting the UI.

// App.jsx — one import per component import Navbar from "./components/Navbar/Navbar"; import Sidebar from "./components/Sidebar/Sidebar"; import Notes from "./components/Notes/Notes"; function App() { return ( <div> <Navbar /> <Sidebar /> <Notes notes={notes} /> </div> ); } // each component — its own folder + file export default function Navbar() { return <nav>...</nav>; }
rule: one component per file·compose in App.jsx
05/08
~/day43$cat Navbar.jsx Navbar.css
[ CONCEPT ] STYLING IN REACT

CSS per component.

// Navbar.jsx — import the CSS file import "./Navbar.css"; function Navbar() { return ( <nav className="navbar"> <span className="logo-text">Keep</span> </nav> ); }
/* Navbar.css */ nav { display: flex; height: 64px; border-bottom: 1px solid #dadce0; }
key: className not class · import .css directly
06/08
~/day43$cat Notes.jsx Note.jsx
[ CONCEPT ] PROPS & DYNAMIC DATA

Data flows down.

// App.jsx — data defined, passed as prop const notes = [{ title: "Buy milk", text: "2% please" }]; <Notes notes={notes} />
// Notes.jsx — receives & passes to Note export default function Notes(props) { return <Note title={props.notes[0].title} text={props.notes[0].text} />; }
// Note.jsx — renders with { } export default function Note(props) { return ( <div><div>{props.title}</div> <div>{props.text}</div></div> ); }
flow: App → Notes → Note·one-way data
